1-expert.prompt_text: Search Condition resources with SNOMED 6142004 (Influenza). Query: Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|6142004

1-naive.raw_response:
Here are FHIR queries to find patients with influenza (the flu), using both SNOMED CT and ICD-10-CM codes:

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|6142004&_include=Condition:patient
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|J09,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|J10,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|J11&_include=Condition:patient
Condition?code:text=influenza&_include=Condition:patient
```

### Code Reference:

| System | Code | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| SNOMED CT | `6142004` | Influenza (disorder) |
| ICD-10-CM | `J09` | Influenza due to certain identified influenza viruses |
| ICD-10-CM | `J10` | Influenza due to other identified influenza virus |
| ICD-10-CM | `J11` | Influenza due to unidentified influenza virus |

### Notes:
- `_include=Condition:patient` pulls in the related **Patient** resources in the same response bundle.
- The **text search** (`code:text=influenza`) is a fallback for records using free-text or non-standard coding.
- You can add `&clinical-status=active` to filter for **currently active** flu diagnoses only.

1-broad.raw_response:
Here are the FHIR queries to find patients diagnosed with influenza (acute viral respiratory infection), using both SNOMED CT and ICD-10-CM coding systems:

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|6142004&_include=Condition:patient

Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|J09.X2,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|J10.1,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|J11.1&_include=Condition:patient
```

---

### 🔍 Code Reference

| System | Code | Display |
|---|---|---|
| SNOMED CT | `6142004` | Influenza (disorder) |
| ICD-10-CM | `J09.X2` | Influenza – novel identified influenza A virus, other respiratory |
| ICD-10-CM | `J10.1` | Influenza – other identified influenza virus, other respiratory |
| ICD-10-CM | `J11.1` | Influenza – unidentified influenza virus, other respiratory |

---

### 📝 Notes
- **`_include=Condition:patient`** pulls the linked `Patient` resources in one request.
- The ICD-10-CM query uses a **comma-separated list** to match any of the three influenza respiratory codes in a single call.
- Add **`&clinical-status=active`** to filter for currently active diagnoses only.
- Add **`&onset-date=ge2026-01-01`** to scope results to a specific season or time range.
1-broad.prompt_text: Find patients diagnosed with influenza — acute viral respiratory infection.

1-expert.raw_response:
## FHIR Query: Condition by SNOMED Code (Influenza)

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|6142004
```

### Query Breakdown

| Component | Value | Description |
|-----------|-------|-------------|
| **Resource** | `Condition` | Clinical condition/diagnosis resource |
| **Parameter** | `code` | Searches the condition's coded value |
| **System** | `http://snomed.info/sct` | SNOMED CT terminology system |
| **Code** | `6142004` | SNOMED concept for **Influenza** |

### Notes
- This query returns all `Condition` resources coded with **SNOMED CT 6142004 (Influenza)**
- The `code` search parameter is a **token** type and matches on `system|code`
- To broaden results, consider using `:text` modifier or adding equivalent codes:
  - **ICD-10-CM**: `J09.X2`, `J10.*`, `J11.*`
  - **ICD-9-CM**: `487.x`

### Optional Enhancements
```
# Include patient information in results
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|6142004&_include=Condition:patient

# Filter by active conditions only
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|6142004&clinical-status=active

# Filter by recent onset (last 30 days)
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|6142004&onset-date=ge2026-05-03
```

1-naive.raw_response:
Here are the FHIR queries to find patients on Tamiflu (oseltamivir), searching across relevant medication resources:

```
MedicationRequest?medication.code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|318198&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
MedicationStatement?medication.code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|318198&status=active&_include=MedicationStatement:patient
MedicationAdministration?medication.code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|318198&_include=MedicationAdministration:patient
```

**Notes:**
| Detail | Value |
|---|---|
| Drug Name | Tamiflu (oseltamivir) |
| RxNorm Code | `318198` (oseltamivir ingredient) |
| Resources Searched | MedicationRequest, MedicationStatement, MedicationAdministration |
| `_include` | Pulls related `Patient` resources in the same response |

- **`318198`** is the RxNorm ingredient-level code for oseltamivir, which will match all formulations (capsules, oral suspension, etc.)
- Remove `&status=active` if you want historical/completed prescriptions as well
- Some systems may use product-level RxNorm codes (e.g., `352051` for 75mg capsule); adjust if needed based on your system's coding granularity

1-broad.raw_response:
## FHIR Queries: Oseltamivir (Tamiflu) Prescriptions

```
MedicationRequest?medication.code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|352008&_include=MedicationRequest:patient&_include=MedicationRequest:medication
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|352008&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1236631&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
MedicationStatement?medication.code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|352008&_include=MedicationStatement:patient
MedicationStatement?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|352008&_include=MedicationStatement:patient
```

---

### 📋 Key Details

| Element | Value |
|---|---|
| **Drug Name** | Oseltamivir (brand: Tamiflu) |
| **Drug Class** | Neuraminidase inhibitor antiviral |
| **Primary RxNorm (ingredient)** | `352008` — oseltamivir |
| **RxNorm (75mg capsule)** | `1236631` — oseltamivir 75 MG Oral Capsule |
| **Indication** | Influenza A & B treatment/prophylaxis |

### 🔍 Query Strategy Notes
- **`352008`** is the **ingredient-level** code and will capture all formulations/strengths
- **`1236631`** targets the most common dispensed form (75mg capsule)
- Both **`MedicationRequest`** (orders) and **`MedicationStatement`** (reported use) are queried for completeness
- `_include` directives pull linked **Patient** and **Medication** resources in a single request
- To filter by **active prescriptions only**, append `&status=active`
- To filter by **recent prescriptions** (e.g., flu season), append `&authoredon=ge2026-01-01`
1-broad.prompt_text: Find patients prescribed oseltamivir (Tamiflu) — antiviral neuraminidase inhibitor used to treat influenza.

1-expert.raw_response:
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|260101
```

### Query Breakdown

| Component | Value | Notes |
|-----------|-------|-------|
| **Resource** | `MedicationRequest` | Prescription/medication order records |
| **Parameter** | `code` | Medication code identifier |
| **System** | `http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm` | RxNorm terminology system |
| **Code** | `260101` | Oseltamivir (Tamiflu) |

### Clinical Context
- **Drug**: **Oseltamivir (Tamiflu)** — neuraminidase inhibitor antiviral
- **Primary Use**: Treatment & prophylaxis of **Influenza A & B**
- **Influenza Specificity**: ✅ **Highly specific** — prescribed almost exclusively for influenza
- **Typical Use Cases**:
  - Acute influenza treatment (within 48 hrs of symptom onset)
  - Post-exposure prophylaxis in high-risk patients
  - Outbreak management in care facilities

### Recommended Extensions
```
# Include patient demographics
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|260101&_include=MedicationRequest:patient

# Filter active prescriptions only
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|260101&status=active

# Seasonal surveillance window (flu season)
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|260101&authoredon=ge2025-10-01&authoredon=le2026-03-31
```
1-expert.prompt_text: Search MedicationRequest with RxNorm 260101 (oseltamivir). Highly influenza-specific. Query: MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|260101

2-naive.raw_response:
The server uses RxNorm at the **ingredient level** — all 3 sampled records carry code `260101` (oseltamivir), and the query returns **431 results**. That's a clean, plausible count. The VSAC value set confirms `260101` is the root ingredient code covering all formulations (75 mg capsule, 30/45 mg variants, 6 mg/mL suspension — all branded as Tamiflu).

---

**Final query:**

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|260101
```

**Why this works:**
| Decision | Rationale |
|---|---|
| `MedicationRequest` | Server prescriptions live here; all 3 samples confirmed this resource type |
| RxNorm `260101` (oseltamivir) | Ingredient-level code — the server codes **all** Tamiflu formulations to this single RxNorm concept; confirmed in every sampled record |
| No dose-form codes needed | FHIR servers using ingredient-level RxNorm don't separately record the ~31 dose-form/strength variants; one code catches all |
| Count = 431 | Plausible for a real EHR dataset with seasonal flu prescribing |

2-broad.raw_response:
The count stays at **431 regardless** — the server encodes all prescriptions at the **ingredient level (RxNorm `260101`)**, so the ingredient-level code is perfectly sufficient. The full VSAC code list adds zero additional records on this server.

---

## 🔍 Analysis Summary

| Signal | Finding |
|---|---|
| **FHIR Server** | Azure FHIR R4 (4.0.1) |
| **Medication code system** | RxNorm exclusively (confirmed by 3 sampled records) |
| **Server-side coding granularity** | Ingredient-level: RxNorm `260101` (oseltamivir) |
| **VSAC value set** | 32-code set covering all strengths, dose forms & brand names |
| **Ingredient code alone** | Captures all 431 MedicationRequests — adding formulation codes adds 0 new records |
| **Key RxNorm codes in value set** | `260101` (ingredient) · `259275` (phosphate salt) · `312122` (75mg cap) · `728109`/`728113` (30/45mg caps) · `314157` (oral suspension) · `261657` (Tamiflu brand) |

---

## ✅ Final FHIR Query

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|260101,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|259275,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1546279,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|312122,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|261315,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|374615,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|728109,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|728111,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|728113,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|728115,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|314157,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|375196,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1115698,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1115700,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|261657,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|581625
```

> **Why include all formulation codes despite the same count?** For **portability**: on a different FHIR server (Epic, Cerner, etc.) that stores meds at the clinical-drug level (e.g., "oseltamivir 75 MG Oral Capsule"), the ingredient-only code would miss records. Including all VSAC value-set codes (ingredient + salt + dose-form + brand) ensures the query is correct across any real-world EHR system.

If you need **formulation-level** specificity (e.g., 75 mg capsule), you may want to also consider RxNorm clinical drug codes like `311782` (oseltamivir 75 mg oral capsule), but `260101` captures all oseltamivir prescriptions at the ingredient level.
